taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Data Analysis, Data Scientist
core_skills: Data Science, Machine Learning, Algorithm Development, Time-Series Data Analysis, Sensor Data Analysis
skill_match_scores: Data Science:1.0, Machine Learning:1.0, Algorithm Development:1.0, Time-Series Data Analysis:0.5, Sensor Data Analysis:0.0
must_have_quals: MSc degree with 2+ years of relevant industry experience or a PhD in a related field (e.g., data science, biomechanics, signal processing, or machine learning), Experience developing and deploying data science or machine learning solutions to production as part of a cross-functional team, Hands-on experience working with time-series data from multi-axis accelerometers, gyroscopes, or similar MEMS sensors, ideally in wearables, biomechanics, or motion analysis contexts, Strong understanding of sensor data characteristics, noise sources, and real-world constraints, and experience designing algorithms that are robust to these challenges, Expert-level proficiency in Python, including deep knowledge of scientific/ML libraries, and experience building robust, scalable, and production-ready code, Expert-level proficiency in SQL and data wrangling, and comfort contributing to and maintaining the data pipelines for the features you develop, A solid understanding of experimental design, causal inference, and validation methodologies for ML-powered product features, Familiarity with end-to-end development using AWS (or a similar cloud service), Experience working with international and distributed teams, Prior experience in the athletic performance technology space or wearable sensing
preferred_quals: A background in biomechanics, kinesiology, or sports science, Experience with MLOps principles and tools for model deployment and monitoring, Experience with hardware development, Experience with deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow)
core_responsibilities: Develop Movement Algorithms: Contribute to the research, prototyping, and development of movement and activity algorithms, with a focus on leveraging existing and emerging hardware sensing capabilities, Translate Sensor Data into Insights: Design, build, and deploy production-grade models and analytical pipelines that translate raw sensor data into accurate and actionable movement metrics and personalized insights for Oura members, Collaborate on HardwareAlgorithm Co-Design: Partner closely with Hardware, Firmware, and Engineering teams to evaluate new sensing modalities, understand sensor limitations and tradeoffs, and influence how hardware capabilities translate into reliable movement algorithms, Influence Product Direction: Collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Design to shape the technical vision and roadmap for movement sensing and tracking, ensuring solutions are scientifically sound and deliver meaningful member value
job_summary: ŌURA is a company dedicated to empowering individuals to understand and improve their health through innovative products. They are seeking a Data Scientist to develop movement algorithms and translate sensor data into actionable insights, collaborating closely with hardware and engineering teams to enhance product offerings.
